title: Cross-Encoder Rerankers Reliably Improve Code Localization
claim: Off-the-shelf cross-encoder rerankers, applied as a post-retrieval rerank over a clean structural-retrieval candidate set, reliably improve repository-level code localization for coding-agent context retrieval. This tests the reranker's marginal effect on a no-rerank structural base; it is NOT a test of the shipped in-pipeline fusion rerank.
scope: File-localization and tokens-to-first-correct-file on commit-derived code tasks, measured as a post-retrieval cross-encoder rerank over the top-20 structural candidates. The baseline is structural retrieval WITHOUT any reranker (reranker=null) — deliberately not the shipped 'structural' default, which fuses a deterministic reranker in-pipeline via HybridRetriever. This claim does not assert end-to-end edit success.

priorArt.title: CORE-Bench / code retrieval benchmark work
priorArt.note: Current code-retrieval work increasingly evaluates retrieval plus reranking; use the formal candidate-discovery pass to replace this placeholder with exact primary citations.

priorArt.title: General RAG reranker practice
priorArt.note: Engineering practice commonly treats cross-encoder reranking as a quality upgrade; this manifest tests whether that assumption survives realistic code-localization tasks.

positiveControl.hypothesis: An oracle reranker that promotes known relevant files already present in the top-20 candidate window improves both Hits@1 and tokens-to-first-correct-file (its Hits@1 ceiling = base recall@20), proving the harness can detect a reranking effect on the REAL tasks being claimed about — not a hand-crafted easy set.
positiveControl.command: npx tsx benchmarks/evaluate.ts --dataset /tmp/spacefolding-reranker-tasks.json --corpus /path/to/corpus --strategy structural-plain --symbol-removed --json > /tmp/spacefolding-reranker-control-plain.json && npx tsx benchmarks/evaluate.ts --dataset /tmp/spacefolding-reranker-tasks.json --corpus /path/to/corpus --strategy structural-rerank-oracle --symbol-removed --json > /tmp/spacefolding-reranker-control-oracle.json && npx tsx benchmarks/reranker-claim-report.ts --baseline /tmp/spacefolding-reranker-control-plain.json --candidate /tmp/spacefolding-reranker-control-oracle.json --manifest benchmarks/claims/reranker-reliability.json --require-confirm --json > /tmp/spacefolding-reranker-control-report.json
positiveControl.passCriterion: The paired control report is valid, oracle reranking improves both Hits@1 and tokens-to-first-correct-file with the paired CI excluding zero, and Recall@10 does not regress on the same tasks. A near-zero oracle gain is informative about base-retrieval rank distribution, not a harness failure.

realismGate.regime: Commit-derived django, typescript, and rust tasks with symbol-removed ablations and at least one large-corpus scale case.
realismGate.passCriterion: Cross-encoder reranking improves both Hits@1 and tokens-to-first-correct-file with the paired CI excluding zero, without a paired-CI Recall@10 regression, in the declared regimes.

killCriterion: Debunk the reliability claim if, in ANY declared regime (django/typescript/rust and the scale case), cross-encoder reranking fails to improve either required improvement metric (Hits@1 and tokens-to-first-correct-file) with the paired CI excluding zero, OR regresses Recall@10 with the paired CI excluding zero. The verdict is machine-evaluated from claimCriteria via the paired claim report; perRegimeAggregation 'all' means every regime must confirm.

artifacts.purpose: Paired positive-control report comparing structural-plain and oracle reranking.
Paired-CI claim report comparing plain and reranked arms (commit-derived regimes).
Paired-CI claim report for the scale case referenced by the kill criterion.

verdict.summary: Candidate selected as paper #2. No claim has been run or accepted yet.
